meaning: vt.问候,向...致意,获悉(消息),映入眼帘
examples: She floated down the steps to greet us.
He bounced across the room to greet them.
He rushed to greet her, sweeping his arms wide.
Thompson brought his non-campaign to Capitol Hill for a closed-door meet-and-greet with Republican lawmakers.
This is a neighbourhood place, where many of the same swimmers greet each other daily.
